narrative:
Kylian Mbappé is a centre-forward for France, demonstrating an elite overall rating of 8.15/10. This places him in the top 1% of centre-forwards at this tournament. Notably, 77% of this rating is derived from his consistent club form over the last two seasons.

At this World Cup, Mbappé boasts an impressive 1.429 goals per 90 minutes and 0.408 assists per 90 minutes. He significantly overperforms his expected metrics, with 0.827 expected goals and 0.172 expected assists per 90 minutes. While contributing 4.898 shots and 2.449 key passes per 90 minutes, his 0.204 tackles per 90 minutes indicate a limited defensive role.

Mbappé is clearly the focal point of France's attack, driving both their scoring and creative output. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his remarkable efficiency in converting chances and his ability to exceed expected metrics. His club record of 0.886 goals per 90 minutes over the last two seasons confirms his sustained world-class attacking threat.
